headline: vec_div surfaces FP64 division codegen gap — _nvptx_binop_mnemonic lacks div / `/` case, falls through to honest stub, %fd18 uninitialized read on store
predecessor: rfc071_p4_n50_vec_mul_e2e_2026_05_21 (N50 vec_mul first non-add kernel PASS, commit 097451f3)

context.ptx_size_delta_vs_n50: 0 bytes (identical shape, stub comment is same byte length as the mul.f64 instruction it replaced)
context.driver_version_path: /usr/local/cuda + driver 580 family (see prior fire infra reference)
context.registers_delta_vs_n50: -6 (16 -> 10; ptxas dead-store-eliminated several regs once it saw %fd18 was never assigned a meaningful value)

kernel_shape_delta.from_n50: @gpu_kernel fn vec_mul(a:[f64], b:[f64], c:[f64], n:i64) { ... c[i] = a[i] * b[i] }
kernel_shape_delta.to_n56: @gpu_kernel fn vec_div(a:[f64], b:[f64], c:[f64], n:i64) { ... c[i] = a[i] / b[i] }
deltas: everything else identical (4 params, bounds check, IndexGet/IndexSet, to_i64 gid widen)

codegen_gap_diagnosis.claim: F-RFC071-E2E-VEC-DIV-NUMERIC-EQ FAIL is caused by _nvptx_binop_mnemonic returning empty string for op == 'div' / '/'
codegen_gap_diagnosis.source_location: compiler/codegen/nvptx_target.hexa: fn _nvptx_binop_mnemonic(op: string) -> string  (lines 460-482 at HEAD e4f79e26)
codegen_gap_diagnosis.downstream_emit: fn _nvptx_emit_stmt at lines 813-862: when mnem == "" the codegen emits the honest stub `// RFC 055 055-P0 - unsupported binop: <op>` AND skips the binop instruction entirely. The dst register (%fd18) is still pre-declared in Pass 2 (because dst classification ran before the body branch), so the subsequent st.global.f64 reads it as an undefined value.
codegen_gap_diagnosis.ptxas_behavior: ptxas does NOT reject uninitialized-register-read in PTX — PTX is a virtual ISA, and reading an undefined register yields an unspecified value. The kernel still launches. This is why F-RFC071-NVPTX-E2E-PTXAS-CLEAN and F-RFC071-NVPTX-E2E-LAUNCH both PASS while numeric-eq FAILs.
codegen_gap_diagnosis.what_user_sees: byte_mismatch=1024/1024, max_abs ~ 1.99 (the full range of garbage), c[0] = 7.29e-304 (denormal trash bit-pattern from a register that previously held something unrelated).

precision_policy_followon_cycle.observation: Since codegen does not emit div at all, this fire could not measure which mode (div.rn.f64 vs div.approx.f64) hexa SHOULD produce. The policy question is pre-empted by the missing op.
precision_policy_followon_cycle.recommended_default: div.rn.f64 (IEEE round-to-nearest, ~52-bit) — matches FP64 user-expectation; mul/add already round-to-nearest by default. Symmetry with add.f64/sub.f64/mul.f64 (no explicit `.rn` suffix because round-to-nearest is the default rounding mode for these ops; PTX ISA §9.7.1.2 add, §9.7.1.3 mul). div is special: it REQUIRES an explicit rounding suffix per PTX ISA §9.7.1.4 — you cannot write bare `div.f64`.
precision_policy_followon_cycle.future_strict_fp_annotation: If a `@strict_fp` (IEEE-compliant) annotation lands, it stays default-on; an `@fast_fp` annotation would opt-in to div.approx.f64 for inverse hot-paths.
precision_policy_followon_cycle.minimal_fix: Add ONE line to _nvptx_binop_mnemonic: `if op == "div" || op == "/" { return "div.rn.f64" }`. Plus the 3 mixed-precision siblings if RFC 068 P3 wants them: div_f16 / div_bf16 / div_f32. The kind-aware wrapper _nvptx_binop_mnemonic_for_kind does NOT need editing (integer div is a follow-up — PTX integer div is div.s64 / div.u64, no rounding-suffix needed).

honest_scope_g3.no_compiler_edits: This fire is artifact-only per the task constraints. The fixture compiler/codegen/nvptx_p6_vec_div_test.hexa is a new file and does not touch any production codegen module. The codegen gap is reported, not patched.
honest_scope_g3.ptx_emit_provenance: Built /tmp/hexa_n56_driver fresh from current self/main.hexa via cp hexa.real -> /tmp/hexac shim (per reference_hexa_basename_sigkill_workaround_2026_05_19). Driver size 1552128 B (similar to N50). HEXA_MODULE_LOADER + HEXA_MAC_BUILD_OK + HEXA_LANG set per reference_hexa_module_loader_env_2026_05_20.
honest_scope_g3.uncommitted_staged_diff_note: On entry, working tree had a large staged diff including compiler/codegen/nvptx_target.hexa (+874 lines) and metal_target.hexa (+532 lines) from a concurrent session. Per memory project_pr_ceremony_freeze_window + feedback_subagent_worktree_leak_pattern, NO files in compiler/ were touched by this cycle. Only the new fixture nvptx_p6_vec_div_test.hexa was added and the artifact dir populated.
honest_scope_g3.fp64_div_semantics_caveat: The CPU reference uses `ha[i] / hb[i]` which is IEEE round-to-nearest on x86_64 (gcc default, no -ffast-math). If hexa codegen had emitted div.rn.f64 we would have expected max_ulp=0 byte-eq. If it had emitted div.approx.f64 we would have expected max_ulp <= ~4 (PTX ISA §9.7.1.4 specifies div.approx.f32 is 2 ULP; div.approx.f64 doesn't exist as a native ISA op — it would need to be synthesized from div.approx.f32 + refinement, which is why div.rn.f64 is the only sensible default).
honest_scope_g3.no_div_approx_f64: Per PTX ISA §9.7.1.4 (PTX ISA 8.7): div.full.f32 / div.approx.f32 exist; div.full.f64 / div.approx.f64 do NOT. The ONLY FP64 div instruction is div.rn.f64. So the precision-policy question for FP64 is moot — there is one mode. (FP32 has the policy choice; FP64 does not.) This is a useful honest correction to the task's framing.
honest_scope_g3.ulp_overflow_meaning: max_ulp = 4.5e18 is essentially uint64_t max — it means the bit patterns are unrelated (one is a tiny denormal, the other is a normal in [0.5, 2)). This is consistent with reading an uninitialized register.
